sections.opening: Sit down, my dear, the candle burns and the night is deep. How many times have you come to me with a dream about mother, about a parental figure, about embracing arms or a comforting voice? These are among the deepest and most poignant dreams we have. Our mother in dreams is not just the physical person - she is all the femininity within us, all the nurturing and protection we need. And how do we read these symbols? How do we understand what the soul is trying to tell us through this figure? Tonight we will learn to decipher every symbol of mother, womb, home and nurturing that emerges from the depths of the subconscious.
sections.theoryA:
What Does the Mother Symbol Mean in Dreams?

The mother figure in dreams represents the archetype of the Great Mother - the nurturer, protector, life-giver. When mother appears in a dream, she brings messages about your need for care, connection to roots, and the tender part of you that wants to grow.

In Jewish tradition, the mother in dreams is connected to the Shekhinah - the feminine presence of the divine. The sages said that when you dream of a deceased mother who comes to announce something, this is a prophetic dream that requires attention. The Talmud teaches us that a mother in a dream can be a messenger from our soul.

Kabbalah connects the mother figure to the sefirah of Binah - the supernal understanding that comprehends and nourishes. When you see mother in a dream, it may be a sign that this sefirah is awakening in you, that you're connecting to the deep feminine wisdom within you.

In the Jungian approach, the mother represents the archetypal mother that lives in all of us - men and women alike. She brings messages about creativity, unconditional love, protection, but also about excessive control or dependency that needs to be released.

practice1.instruction: Think back to a recent dream where you saw a mother (yours or any mother). Write 3 words that describe how you felt when you saw her. What was the first word that came to mind when you woke up?

sections.theoryB:
How to Interpret Symbols of Home and Mother's Embrace?

The home in dreams is always a symbol of the soul, and the kitchen is the heart - the place where mother nurtures and nourishes. When you dream of mother in the kitchen, it means the soul needs emotional care, that there's a part of you that wants to be pampered and fed.

Mother's embrace in a dream is the safest place that exists. In Islamic tradition, Ibn Sirin interprets dreaming of mother's embrace as a sign of security, success in matters, and blessing to come. If mother hugs you in a dream - it's a sign that you're protected from evil eyes.

In Kabbalah, the embrace connects to the sefirah of Chesed - the flow of love given without return. When you dream of a warm embrace, it's a sign that this sefirah is open in your life, that you're in a place of healthy receiving and giving.

In numerology there's also a connection: if you were born on the 2nd, 6th, or 9th, these symbols will be many times stronger in your dreams. Number 2 connects to nurturing and partnership, 6 to family love, and 9 to a new beginning growing in security.

practice2.instruction: Close your eyes and imagine: you're standing in your mother's kitchen. What do you see? What smell? What sound? Write this experience in one paragraph, then find the strongest symbol that came to you.

sections.deepDive:
What's Not Written in Books - When Mother Dies in a Dream

This is the greatest difficulty in dream interpretation - when you see mother dying, or when a deceased mother comes in a dream. What's not written in any book: this is not a vision of the future. It's never bad.

In Jewish tradition there's a clear rule: when the deceased comes in a dream and speaks, it's good tidings. A mother dying in a dream represents the end of an old stage in your life - childhood, dependency, fear. It's a rebirth of your feminine power.

In Kabbalistic wisdom, this is called 'death of the vessels' - breaking of old forms so new light can enter. When mother 'dies' in a dream, the dependent part of you is released, and the independent part is born.

But if a deceased mother comes to you in a dream and speaks - pay very close attention to her words. Sometimes she comes to heal, to forgive, or to convey an important message that your soul needs to hear. This isn't fear - it's grace.

questions.question: How do we interpret a dream of mother in the kitchen according to Kabbalistic tradition?
options: The need for emotional nurturing and warm memory
A sign of financial wealth to come
A connection to the past that should be forgotten
questions.explanation: The kitchen is the heart of the home and place of nurturing, so dreaming of mother in the kitchen symbolizes a need for emotional nourishment and care

questions.question: What does mother's embrace represent in a dream according to Ibn Sirin?
questions.explanation: In Islamic tradition, mother's embrace symbolizes security, success and abundance coming into life

questions.question: Which Kabbalistic sefirah connects to the embrace symbol?
questions.explanation: The embrace connects to the sefirah of Chesed - the flow of love given without return

questions.question: What's the meaning when a deceased mother comes and speaks in a dream?
options: A bad sign requiring preparation for troubles
Good tidings and an important message from the soul
A memory that should be erased
questions.explanation: In Jewish tradition, when the deceased comes in a dream and speaks it's always good tidings and an important message

questions.question: Which numerological numbers strengthen mother symbols in dreams?
questions.explanation: Number 2 (nurturing and partnership), 6 (family love) and 9 (new beginning in security) connect to mother energy

closing.summary: Mother symbols in dreams are among the deepest. The mother represents nurturing, security, roots, and the feminine power within us all. When she appears in a dream - she brings a message about what your soul needs right now.
closing.wisdom: The best mother you have lives inside your heart - always there, always nurturing.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll learn about water symbols in dreams - the sea, rain, and springs that bring messages from the depths...

faq.question: What if I dream of a mother I never knew in life?
faq.answer: This is the archetypal mother - the universal nurturing figure that lives in every soul. She represents the gentle and caring side of yourself.

faq.question: Why do I always dream that my mother is angry with me?
faq.answer: An angry mother in dreams usually symbolizes inner criticism or guilt about some decision. She's inviting you to forgiveness - of yourself.

faq.question: Is dreaming of birth connected to mother symbols?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Birth in dreams symbolizes new creation, a new project, or your own rebirth. It's a symbol of feminine potential being realized.

faq.question: What does it mean when I see myself as a mother in a dream?
faq.answer: It's a sign that your nurturing power is awakening. You might be entering a new role of giving and caring - not necessarily children, but also projects or people.

crossReferences.label: 🌛 Moon in Astrology - The Maternal Side

seo.title: Dream Interpretation: Mother Symbols and Embrace in Dreams - Maktuba
seo.description: Learn how to interpret mother symbols in dreams: embrace, home, nurturing and messages from the depths. Jewish and Kabbalistic dream interpretation with practical explanations and exercises.
